skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review web UI files against Web Interface Guidelines, including accessibility, UX, and implementation quality checks.
skill.summary: Audit web interface code against current design and accessibility guidelines.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks, frontmatter text, and a sync hash. The skill does request a live WebFetch from raw.githubusercontent.com and asks the model to follow fetched output instructions, which creates a mutable external instruction dependency.

medium_findings.title: Mutable External Instructions Fetched at Runtime
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the agent to fetch fresh guidelines from a GitHub raw URL and says the fetched content contains output format instructions. This is a legitimate design-review workflow, but it lets external mutable content influence model behavior during each review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network URL and instruction to use fetched rules are explicit. I did not find evidence of credential access, exfiltration, or malicious intent, so the risk is medium.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives. The backticks appear in markdown code formatting and a fenced URL block, with no executable script or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown text, not Ruby or shell code. No executable file, command runner, or interpolation context is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings are not cryptographic code. They match frontmatter text, normal prose, or a Git commit SHA used as sync metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain skill metadata, user-facing instructions, and a source path. I found no hashing, encryption, password storage, or crypto API use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External WebFetch Controls Review Rules
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on WebFetch from a mutable raw GitHub URL before each review. Marketplace users should understand that remote content may change after publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external fetch requirement is clearly documented. The impact is limited because the URL is public guideline content and no secret access is requested.

content.user_title: Audit Web UI Against Design Guidelines
content.value_statement: Frontend reviews often miss accessibility, interaction, and visual consistency issues. This skill applies Web Interface Guidelines to selected files and returns concise file-line findings.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the latest Web Interface Guidelines from the configured source.
Reviews user-specified files or file patterns for guideline compliance.
Checks UI code for accessibility, usability, and interface quality issues.
Reports findings in a concise file-line format.
Asks which files to review when no target is provided.
limitations: Requires network access to fetch the current guideline source.
Can only review files that the AI tool can read in the workspace.
Does not run browser-based accessibility tests by itself.
Remote guideline changes can alter review criteria over time.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request UI Change
use_cases.description: Check changed frontend files before merging a visual or interaction update.

use_cases.title: Audit Accessibility Before Release
use_cases.description: Find interface issues that may affect keyboard use, labels, contrast, or structure.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design QA
use_cases.description: Apply the same interface guidelines across multiple projects or teams.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single UI file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review src/components/Header.tsx against the Web Interface Guidelines and report only actionable findings.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Feature Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit src/features/settings/**/* for accessibility, UX, and visual consistency issues using the Web Interface Guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Review Changed UI Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Pre-merge review focused on recent work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the UI files changed in this branch against the Web Interface Guidelines. Prioritize issues that could block release.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Against Product Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review with extra project context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the checkout UI against the Web Interface Guidelines and our existing design patterns. Separate guideline violations from consistency concerns.

output_examples.input: Review a navigation component for guideline compliance.
output: Header component: missing keyboard-visible focus state on navigation links.
Mobile menu: close control needs an accessible label.
Active route styling is only color-based and needs a secondary visual cue.

output_examples.input: Audit a settings page before release.
output: Settings form: destructive action is too close to the primary save action.
Notification toggles: labels should describe the resulting state.
Error messages need to be associated with their related fields.

best_practices: Review a focused set of UI files for clearer findings.
Run the skill before final design QA or release review.
Ask for severity grouping when reviewing large interface changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only accessibility validation step.
Do not review generated build output instead of source files.
Do not ignore remote guideline changes between repeated audits.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. It reviews files and reports findings unless you separately ask for fixes.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to fetch the latest guidelines from a GitHub raw URL.

faq.question: Can it review a whole application?
faq.answer: Yes, but smaller file sets usually produce clearer and more actionable results.

faq.question: Does it replace automated accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. Use it with browser testing, linters, and manual assistive technology checks.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill fetches mutable external instructions at runtime, even though no malicious behavior was found.
